zotero.abstractNote: Next-Best-View (NBV) planning is a fundamental problem in robotics, where the goal is to select a sequence of sensing actions to minimize uncertainty about the environment. In this paper, we consider the problem of multi-sensor NBV planning, where a robot can choose between different sensors at each step. We show that the multi-sensor NBV problem can be formulated as the maximization of a submodular set function subject to a matroid constraint. This formulation allows us to use a greedy algorithm with a provable approximation guarantee of 1-1/e. We evaluate our approach in simulation and on a real robot, showing that it outperforms existing methods in terms of both information gain and computational efficiency.
